Hello everyone and welcome to Super Lean for Halloween, Lean for Halloween, Lean by Halloween. Any of those variations would work. I think if it were me, I would choose Lean by Halloween, but it is totally up to you. So what you can see here inside of the seasonal plays, this is one of the seasonal plays depending on when you're watching this, there may be others because we will continue to update this, but any who, what we've done here is put together the email sequences, the text sequences, posters for you, sign up sheets, the actual workout that go along with the six week Halloween, Lean by Halloween challenge. And essentially, all we're doing here is from a hook standpoint, we're going to give them a Halloween candy that they can swap out for their carb portions. And so what we've done is basically made equivalents, a very long list actually, of all the different Halloween candies and what they can swap out for their carb portions. So that's what makes it interesting and fun. The time of year and the seasonality makes people more likely to sign off work because everyone thinks of what they're going to look like in a costume or at least from members of a time when they wish they had been in a costume that was good. And one of the big offers here that you'll have at the end is that you'll do an event. And so I would imply that if they do it, they can come to the event, but obviously you'll let anyone come. But in the way that the email is worded, it makes it seem like they're getting an event as a part of it, which in and of itself, people don't have many things to do with their lives. And so having an event to look forward to is one of those nice things. So anywho, the play is very straightforward. They're going to run it internally 10 to 14 days, basically the beginning of September. And then you'll have the six weeks prior to the 31st of October for Halloween. And then at that point, just have a party at the end of it, tell them to come in their costumes and either they can take four pictures of them in a costume or you can award winners on stage. There's lots of little things that we have. And just as an overarching description of how this works, very simple. If you want to add a team component, you can, which I'll get to at the end, but costs between $99 and $199, it depends on how sweet you want to make the party at the end. I would give $25 to 50 bucks commissions to the trainers or the salesperson who's going to be selling it from a fulfillment standpoint. The beautiful thing here is that it's essentially a zero fulfillment play. And so only thing you have to do is give them the nutrition plan with the candy slash chocolate nutrition overlays that swaps out the candy for their plan. We'll have a separate Facebook group. You want to explain slash demonstrate 18 of the MetCon workouts, already have them. Basically it's six workouts that they do three times over each time over the six weeks they're going to. So it's like week one and two, they have six workouts. Week three and four, they have six workouts in week five and six, they have the same six workouts, but each time they're going to go up in either weight or reps or they're going to be doing it faster. They do need to take a sweaty selfie to count the workout posted in the group with the time they took it took them to finish it in. Create a sticker board to track the workouts and insert finish times. They have to complete all the workouts to be eligible for the prizes for individual winners. You have a party at the end. Easy thing to do would be to hire or trade with a photographer for pictures at the event or the party at the end, which is cool because people are going to make costumes. It'll be fun. And then you want to give a award, which is the best visual winner gets two free months male and female. Very easy. If you want to make a team component to add to it, this is an added sexifier that actually has no sexual component to it whatsoever. The team component is that you could have two teams and the team with the most stickers slash completed workouts total between all members wins the swag, which would be like shirts or hoodies. I would do hoodies if you're going to do $1.99 price point and you could do t-shirts if you're doing a $9.99 price point. And if both teams have the exact same amounts and every single person had perfect attendance and did all 18 workouts, then take the average of the last workout done for time for each team and use that as the tiebreaker or use the improvement as the tiebreaker, which you can see on the workouts. Those workouts are listed as tiebreaker. It's the last workout of every six workout blocks at the end of week two, the tiebreaker workout and the week four, tiebreaker workout and a week six, their tiebreaker workout. So you can look at the improvement of those and that's only assuming they had perfect attendance because if they didn't have perfect attendance, then you would just give it to the people who had the most workouts completed. So that's the team component. If you want to add it in, if you don't, that's fine too. But teams just always make things more fun. I think. But yeah, this is more or less how it works. It's a very easy play. The workouts are done at home or in off hours. They're purposely made so that their body weight workouts, they don't need to be done at home. The idea is that if we're doubling their activity from three workouts a week to six and we're following a meal plan, then they will be more likely to achieve a huge transformation. And then also with the team component, they'll really be sure to make sure that they're doing it. So they'll post their sweaty selfie after they work out at home. They'll post it in the group. And then you will tag them or you'll put a sticker when they come to the gym. They'll put a sticker on the fact that they get a workout. So very easy to track. And yeah, that's more or less how the play works.
